NVDA.title: Michael Saylor's Strategy Back To Buying Bitcoin After 1-Week Pause? BTC Bull Says 'No More Bears'

macro_events.event_summary: Iran-US tensions escalate as Tehran rejects de-escalation and Trump rebuffs Iran's peace counteroffer, prolonging Middle East conflict with implied risk to Strait of Hormuz oil flows.
primary_drivers: Crude oil price spike on Hormuz transit risk premium
Risk-off rotation into safe havens and away from oil-sensitive consumer names

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with significant upstream leverage; every $10/bbl move in Brent flows directly to upstream earnings. Direct beneficiary of Hormuz risk premium without Iran-exposed assets.
High-beta US shale pure-play (Permian) with above-peer leverage to crude prices; trades as a levered call on oil. No Middle East operational exposure.

Prime contractor for THAAD, PAC-3 Patriot interceptors, and JASSM — all directly consumed/replenished in Middle East air defense and strike operations. Conflict prolongation extends munitions burn rate.
Maker of Patriot missiles, Tomahawks, and AIM-120; Raytheon segment is the most direct munitions-replenishment beneficiary of a prolonged Iran conflict.
B-2/B-21 stealth bomber prime and ICBM/strategic deterrent contractor; benefits from elevated defense spending posture and potential strike-package activity.
TechnipFMC — subsea equipment provider benefits from oil capex pull-forward as majors lock in projects amid sustained high crude; less direct than E&Ps but clear linkage.
Tanker rates spike on Hormuz disruption fears as shippers reroute — though note: pick one tanker name. Actually using FRO instead.
Frontline — VLCC tanker operator; Hormuz transit disruption and rerouting around Cape of Good Hope dramatically increases ton-miles and spot tanker rates.

losers.rationale: American Airlines — jet fuel is ~25% of operating costs and is unhedged; sustained crude spike compresses margins directly. Also exposed to Middle East airspace rerouting on international routes.
United has the largest international/long-haul exposure of US carriers including Middle East/India routes that overfly the region; fuel cost passthrough + rerouting drag.
Carnival — bunker fuel cost sensitivity plus direct itinerary disruption risk for Mediterranean/Middle East cruises which typically rebook at lower margins.
FedEx — jet fuel exposure for Express segment and global air network; rising crude squeezes margins and Middle East hub (Dubai) is operationally affected.
Dollar General — low-income consumer is most sensitive to gasoline price spikes which act as a regressive tax; same-store traffic historically inversely correlates with pump prices.
Delta operates its own refinery (Trainer) providing partial hedge, but still net exposed to jet crack spreads widening on a crude shock.

headlines: Iran says it will 'never bow' as Trump rejects peace counteroffer, prolonging Middle East conflict

macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices rising on escalating Middle East tensions after hawkish remarks from Netanyahu and Trump raise risk of Iran-related conflict.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium in crude prices
Potential supply disruption risk from Strait of Hormuz / Iranian exports
Defense spending tailwind from heightened conflict risk

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated major with significant upstream leverage; ~$1 move in Brent flows directly to E&P earnings. Pure beta to crude price.
High-leverage Permian pure-play with strong torque to WTI; balance sheet still levered means oil price moves amplify equity beta. Buffett-held adds flow support.
E&P with direct Egypt/offshore operations and high crude beta; small-cap E&Ps typically lead the tape on geopolitical oil spikes.
Direct beneficiary of Israel/Middle East tensions — maker of THAAD, PAC-3 Patriot interceptors, F-35s already in heavy demand from Israel and Gulf allies. Escalation = restock orders.
Patriot missile and interceptor producer; Israel and Saudi air-defense restocking pipeline expands on regional conflict risk.
Offshore vessel operator with heavy Middle East/Gulf exposure; day rates and utilization rise with sustained $80+ crude.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25% of operating cost; airlines lack hedging discipline they once had. Direct margin compression from crude spike.
Same fuel cost passthrough mechanism as DAL; international routing also exposed to Middle East airspace disruption rerouting costs.
Domestic carrier with thinnest margin buffer to fuel cost shocks; historically the most fuel-price-sensitive US airline equity.
Jet fuel + diesel exposure across air and ground fleet; fuel surcharges lag the spot move so near-term margin hit.
Bunker fuel is a major cost line and Mediterranean/Red Sea itineraries face rerouting risk if tensions escalate further.

headlines: Oil climbs as Netanyahu and Trump remarks stoke worries over rising Middle East tensions

macro_events.event_summary: Global leaders are closely watching an upcoming Trump-Xi summit, with agenda speculation spanning Iran, Taiwan, and the Strait of Hormuz alongside US-China trade relations.
primary_drivers: Potential US-China tariff/trade de-escalation reducing supply chain risk premium
Geopolitical headline volatility around Taiwan and Iran
Sentiment rotation into China-exposed US equities ahead of summit

beneficiaries.rationale: Boeing aircraft orders from Chinese carriers are a recurring summit deliverable; past Trump-Xi meetings have produced 737/787 order announcements.
Macau gaming revenue ~70% of EBITDA; benefits from any easing of cross-strait/China travel and capital flow rhetoric.
Copper bellwether — a successful summit reduces global growth tail risk and lifts industrial metals on China demand hopes.

losers.rationale: Lockheed's Taiwan arms sales and F-35 program are explicit Chinese demands at any summit; concessions or freezes would hit the Taiwan revenue line and broader China-relations overhang.
Strait of Hormuz is on the agenda — any Iran de-escalation framework reduces the geopolitical risk premium currently embedded in crude.
Domestic-fab semis benefit from US-China tech decoupling; a summit easing of chip export controls would erode the reshoring premium relative to China-exposed peers.

headlines: From Singapore to Brussels, world leaders eye Trump-Xi summit from afar

macro_events.event_summary: China's April CPI and PPI came in hotter than expected, driven by higher energy costs stemming from the ongoing Iran war pushing oil prices up.
primary_drivers: Higher crude oil prices flowing through to global inflation prints
Reduced room for PBoC easing if imported inflation persists
Energy supply risk premium from Middle East conflict

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with significant upstream leverage to Brent; sustained $80+ oil from Iran war supply premium directly lifts E&P earnings and buyback capacity.
Same upstream oil price leverage as XOM; Permian and global upstream production benefits directly from elevated crude driven by Middle East supply risk.
Higher beta to oil price than majors due to Permian-heavy production mix and leverage; benefits disproportionately from sustained crude strength.
Pure-play upstream with no downstream offset; direct earnings sensitivity to Brent prices elevated by Iran conflict supply premium.
Oilfield services activity rises with sustained high oil prices as producers boost capex; China inflation print signals demand isn't collapsing.
Cheniere benefits as Middle East conflict raises premium on non-Gulf LNG supply; European and Asian buyers pay up for US cargoes.

losers.rationale: Domestic airline with limited fuel hedging; sustained higher jet fuel from Iran war crude premium directly compresses operating margins.
Fuel is ~25% of opex; persistent oil strength from Middle East supply risk pressures CASM-ex and Q2 guide.
Naphtha and natural gas liquids feedstock costs rise with crude; commodity chemical margins compressed when end-demand can't absorb passthrough.

headlines: China consumer, wholesale inflation tops estimates in April as Iran war drives energy costs higher
